definition: The quenching or ending of dissatisfaction and its causes.
notes: Project preference is quenching rather than cessation, reflecting the sense of extinguishing a fire or bringing a process to stillness. Ven. Dr. M. Punnaji sometimes uses discontinuity or ending in meditative and awakening contexts; the repository records discontinuity as a source-backed alternate while retaining quenching as the house default. The OSF glossary glosses nirodha as to stop, to cease, or ending of; the repository records stop and cease as source-backed plain-English alternates while keeping quenching as the house default.

context_rules.context: In core doctrinal contexts, especially the four noble truths
In explanatory prose where a plainer English verb is needed
In Punnaji-style meditative explanation of interruption of the existential process
context_rules.notes: Source-backed alternate, not the house default.

context_rules.context: In OSF-glossary style plain-English explanation of the term
context_rules.notes: Source-backed explanatory alternate alongside stop and cease.
